MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-24-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JEFFREY B. WORKMAN, Tel No. (415) 722-1157 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. JOHN F. BAUMGARDNER of Chapman & Intrieri, LLP, Tel No. (510) 864-3600 appearing for Defendant Crunch, LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CRUNCH, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. The cited release is unenforceable as a matter of law. In Leon v. Family Fitness (1998) 61 Cal.App.4th 1227, 1232, the court provides that
"An express release is not enforceable if it is not easily readable." [citation] "Furthermore, the important operative language should be placed in a position which compels notice and must be distinguished from other sections of the document. A [layperson] should not be required to muddle through complex language to know that valuable, legal rights are being relinquished." [citation] An exculpatory clause is unenforceable if not distinguished from other sections, if printed in the same typeface as the remainder of the document, and if not likely to attract attention because it is placed in the middle of a document. [citation] In other words, a release must not be buried in a lengthy document, hidden among other verbiage, or so encumbered with other provisions as to be difficult to find. [citation] In this case, defendant's release is buried in the middle of the membership agreement and not easily noticeable. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
